dealKiller: The candidate's central wedge ('SoftOne/EpsilonNet/Entersoft are ERP record-keepers with bolt-on AI chat — not autonomous ledger agents') is factually wrong as of 2026-Q2. (a) Entersoft and SoftOne MERGED October 2025 into ENTERSOFTONE — 1,500 staff, 700 partners, 90,000+ customers across GR/CY/BG/RO, €137-140M revenue, with 'organic growth and strengthening artificial intelligence at the core of strategy' explicitly per CEO Kotzamanidis. (b) EpsilonNet (1,700 staff, 27,000 accounting professionals as customers, ~€150M 2025 target, ATHEX-listed) has already shipped PYLON Hybrid marketed verbatim as 'Agentic AI ERP' plus Scan4you (AI invoice digitization claiming 92% time savings) plus Digital Accounting (AI-OCR free at every volume) plus dedicated accounting-office task management with native myDATA connector. The two Greek incumbents control the channel, own the data, employ ex-OEE board members as resellers, and are racing the same workflow with deeper distribution and Greek-tax-calendar institutional memory. (c) Cegid acquired Shine Nov 2025 explicitly to build 'Europe's leading financial copilot for SMBs AND accountants' — 1M SMBs, 15,000 accountants, AI-driven, cloud-native, e-invoicing-native — and Greece is a natural next stop given EU e-invoicing harmonization. (d) Pennylane raised $200M+ at $4.25B Jan 2026 with TCV/Blackstone/Sequoia for European expansion (DE live, ES/PL/BE next) — Greek expansion is a 12-18 month away problem at most. The 600-mid-tier-office wedge is already being defended by the incumbent channel.
patternMatches: incumbent-already-shipped: PYLON Hybrid Agentic AI ERP + Scan4you live in market 2025-2026
sovereign-eats-the-input: AADE 2026 e-invoicing mandate produces pre-filled VAT returns and free timologio/myDATAapp — collapses the highest-value workflow into a public good
channel-captured-by-incumbent: ENTERSOFTONE and EpsilonNet OWN the OEE/POFEE reseller network through 20+ years of CPD/training/license bundles; a new SaaS layer cannot rent that channel cheaply
consolidation-wave-ate-the-window: Entersoft+SoftOne merger Oct 2025 and Cegid+Shine merger Nov 2025 both explicitly target the same wedge in 8-month timeframe vs Phase 0 16-week test

cannibalization-fallacy: brief assumes incumbents won't cannibalize per-seat — but EpsilonNet's PYLON Hybrid IS the per-seat-cannibalization product they already shipped; the assumption was load-bearing and false

channel-conflict-on-rev-share: accountant offices DO want to absorb the 5x labor compression themselves as practice growth, not share margin with a SaaS layer (Greek mid-tier offices have grown 33% — they want more files at same headcount, not rev-share)

regulatory-attestation-not-shifted: OEE Resolution 90 frames AI as TOOL for the accountant-of-record — there is no legal architecture in Greek tax law allowing per-file liability transfer to a software vendor; the accountant signs and bears full E&O regardless of who 'drafted' the entry, meaning the 'liability-shifted architecture' moat dissolves on inspection

TAM-compression-from-pre-filled-returns: AADE myDATA v1.0.10 + B2B mandate Feb/Mar 2026 generates pre-filled VAT returns directly — the highest willingness-to-pay line item in the wedge migrates to the sovereign
unitEconomicsConcern: Mid-tier Greek accounting office gross margin 30-50% on bookkeeping; junior at €1,400/mo carries ~€25-30K loaded annual cost servicing ~80 files = €25-30/file/mo true cost. A 5x compression saves €20-24/file/mo. Accountant will pay at most €6-10/file/mo for software (Greek SMB SaaS willingness-to-pay reality) — leaving the office to bank ~€15/file/mo as practice-growth margin, NOT share with vendor. Rev-share thesis is upside-down. Worse: EpsilonNet bundles AI-OCR + myDATA + accounting-office task management inside existing license at zero marginal price to retain accounting-office relationship — effectively price-floor at €0 incremental.
loadBearingAssumption: That Greek incumbents will not ship autonomous ledger agents because per-seat cannibalization protects their P&L. This is empirically falsified: EpsilonNet already shipped PYLON Hybrid 'Agentic AI ERP' explicitly cannibalizing seats, because the alternative was losing the entire accountant relationship to Pennylane/Cegid. The 12-month competitive window assumption is also falsified — they shipped 2025.
alternativeThesis: If T-Life still wants exposure to Greek accountant AI, do NOT build the agent. Two viable alternative angles: (1) Build a narrow myDATA-anomaly-detection + audit-defense product priced as professional-liability-insurance-adjacent, sold to the SAME 600 mid-tier offices but as risk-mitigation NOT labor compression — accountants buy what protects their license, not what compresses their juniors. ~€80-120/office/mo, ~€600-900K ARR ceiling on Greek wedge, expandable to IT/ES on same audit-defense thesis. (2) Build the same agent but sell it as a white-label OEM module licensed TO Entersoftone or EpsilonNet (not against them) — they have channel, you have AI-native execution speed. Test via 8-week scoped POC with 1 of the 2 incumbents; binary outcome on signed OEM term sheet. Either pivot has a real moat (audit-defense data, or being the AI vendor inside the duopoly). The original 'agent does it, accountant signs, we sell direct via OEE' thesis does not survive 2026 reality.
